chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 20: IVD Ecosystem and Community
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the twentieth chapter of the IVD book. Covers the
# IVD ecosystem: community recipes, IDE integration, the IVD MCP server as a
# real-world case study of dogfooding IVD, CLI tools, and future standards.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_4_adoption.chapters[3]
# Source:    mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (MCP server interface),
#            framework.md, templates/intent.yaml
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Present the IVD ecosystem — community recipes, IDE integrations, CLI tools, and the IVD MCP server — with the MCP server as a real-world case study of dogfooding IVD (its own intent artifact with an interface section documenting 15 tools)."

  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Understands the IVD ecosystem as a living community of recipes, tools, and integrations
    2. Knows how to find, use, and contribute community recipes
    3. Can integrate IVD into their IDE of choice (Cursor Rules, Cline Config, Aider, GitHub Copilot)
    4. Understands the IVD MCP server as a real-world case study of dogfooding IVD — its own intent artifact uses the interface section to document 15 tools, making the tool surface verifiable and implementation-independent
    5. Can use the IVD CLI tool to validate, generate, and search intent artifacts
    6. Sees the future roadmap: agent portability, JSON Schema, standards evolution
    7. Feels invited to join the IVD community and contribute their own patterns
    
    This is the ecosystem chapter. The reader transitions from "I can use IVD in my projects" (Parts II–III) to "I can participate in the IVD ecosystem." Prose leads; examples illustrate.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader can navigate the community recipe repository and find relevant patterns
    - Reader can configure IVD in at least one IDE (Cursor, Cline, Aider, or Copilot)
    - Reader understands the IVD MCP server case study: how its own intent artifact documents its 15-tool surface via the interface section (dogfooding)
    - Reader can run basic IVD CLI commands: validate, generate, search
    - Reader has a clear picture of IVD's future direction (standards, portability)
    - Reader knows how to contribute recipes and patterns back to the community
    - Chapter ends with a getting-started checklist for community participation

  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_4 purpose: adoption and scaling)"
    - "mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yam

)
    - Reader understands the IVD MCP server case study: how its own intent artifact documents its 15-tool surface via the interface section (dogfooding)
    - Reader can run basic IVD CLI commands: validate, generate, search
    - Reader has a clear picture of IVD's future direction (standards, portability)
    - Reader knows how to contribute recipes and patterns back to the community
    - Chapter ends with a getting-started checklist for community participation

  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_4 purpose: adoption and scaling)"
    - "mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (MCP server as case study)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch20)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "20.1"
      title: "Community Recipe Repository"
      required_content:
        - "What the recipe repository is: a curated, versioned collection of IVD recipes for common patterns (agent-classifier, workflow-orchestration, infrastructure, integration, etc.)"
        - "How recipes are organized: by pattern type, complexity level, and domain; each recipe has scope, constraints_template, implementation_pattern, verification_pattern, and examples"
        - "How to discover recipes: browsing the repository, using ivd_list_recipes and ivd_load_recipe tools, semantic search via ivd_search"
        - "Quality gates: what makes a recipe canonical vs community-contributed; review process and versioning"
        - "Real example: walk through discovering and loading a recipe for a common problem"
        - "The recipe ecosystem grows as adoption grows — every validated pattern is a potential recipe"

    - id: "20.2"
      title: "Contributing Your Patterns"
      required_content:
        - "When to contribute: you've built something with IVD, validated it across projects, and the pattern generalizes beyond your specific domain"
        - "Recipe anatomy review: scope, constraints_template, implementation_pattern, verification_pattern, examples — what each section must contain"
        - "Contribution workflow: fork → create recipe YAML → validate with ivd_validate → submit PR → community review → merge"
        - "Quality criteria: the recipe must work with at least 2 distinct examples; constraints must be executable (Principle 2); verification must be automated where possible"
        - "Attribution and versioning: recipes carry author metadata, version history, and changelog"
        - "Examples of contributions the community needs: domain-specific recipes (healthcare, fintech, education), language-specific patterns, framework integrations"

    - id: "20.3"
      title: "IDE Integration (Cursor Rules, Cline Config)"
      required_content:
        - "Why IDE integration matters: IVD works best when the AI assistant has IVD context in every conversation — not as a separate step, but as ambient knowledge"
        - "Cursor integration: .cursorrules file that references IVD principles, points to system_intent.yaml, and instructs t

d versioning: recipes carry author metadata, version history, and changelog"
        - "Examples of contributions the community needs: domain-specific recipes (healthcare, fintech, education), language-specific patterns, framework integrations"

    - id: "20.3"
      title: "IDE Integration (Cursor Rules, Cline Config)"
      required_content:
        - "Why IDE integration matters: IVD works best when the AI assistant has IVD context in every conversation — not as a separate step, but as ambient knowledge"
        - "Cursor integration: .cursorrules file that references IVD principles, points to system_intent.yaml, and instructs the AI to use IVD flow (describe → intent → implement → verify)"
        - "Cline integration: cline_docs/ configuration that loads IVD context; how Cline's autonomous mode benefits from intent constraints as guardrails"
        - "Aider integration: .aider.conf.yml and repo-map patterns that expose intent artifacts to Aider's context; conventions files for IVD awareness"
        - "GitHub Copilot integration: .github/copilot-instructions.md for IVD-aware code suggestions; workspace-level prompt customization"
        - "Cross-IDE pattern: regardless of tool, the integration pattern is the same — load IVD context, reference intent artifacts, enforce constraints"
        - "The IVD MCP server as universal bridge: any IDE with MCP support gets all 15 tools without custom configuration"

    - id: "20.4"
      title: "IVD MCP Server (Universal AI Access — Case Study)"
      required_content:
        - "The IVD MCP server as a real-world case study of dogfooding IVD: the server itself has an intent artifact (mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml) that uses the interface section to document its 15-tool surface"
        - "What dogfooding means here: IVD's own tooling is documented using IVD. The interface section makes the tool surface verifiable (Principle 2) and implementation-independent (Principle 7) — the same principles the book teaches"
        - "The 15 tools, organized by category: context (ivd_get_context), recipes (ivd_load_recipe, ivd_list_recipes), templates (ivd_load_template), validation (ivd_validate), scaffolding (ivd_init, ivd_scaffold, ivd_find_artifacts, ivd_check_placement), discovery (ivd_list_features, ivd_discover_goal), analysis (ivd_assess_coverage, ivd_propose_inversions), learning (ivd_teach_concept), search (ivd_search)"
        - "Interface section walkthrough: show how mcp_server_intent.yaml documents each tool with name, description, parameters (name, type, required, description), returns, and test path — making the contract explicit and verifiable"
        - "Architecture: committed embeddings (clone-to-working, no API key to query), dual transport (stdio for local, SSE for remote), self-contained deployment"
        - "Why this matters for adoption: any AI agent with MCP support (Cursor, Claude Desktop, custom agents) gets full IVD access through a standard protocol"
        - "The meta lesson: if your own tools don't use your framework, why should anyone else? Dogfooding is the strongest form of validation"
        - "Constraints from the MCP server intent that demonstrate IVD in practice:

, description), returns, and test path — making the contract explicit and verifiable"
        - "Architecture: committed embeddings (clone-to-working, no API key to query), dual transport (stdio for local, SSE for remote), self-contained deployment"
        - "Why this matters for adoption: any AI agent with MCP support (Cursor, Claude Desktop, custom agents) gets full IVD access through a standard protocol"
        - "The meta lesson: if your own tools don't use your framework, why should anyone else? Dogfooding is the strongest form of validation"
        - "Constraints from the MCP server intent that demonstrate IVD in practice: embeddings_committed, zero_setup_query, self_contained — each with executable test"

    - id: "20.5"
      title: "IVD CLI Tool (Validate, Generate, Search)"
      required_content:
        - "The IVD CLI as the developer's local companion: validate artifacts, scaffold new intents, search the knowledge base — all from the terminal"
        - "ivd validate: check an intent artifact's structure, required sections, constraint completeness; show example output with errors, warnings, suggestions"
        - "ivd init and ivd scaffold: initialize IVD in an existing project (auto-scan and pre-fill); scaffold new intent artifacts in the correct canonical location"
        - "ivd search: semantic search across the IVD knowledge base from the command line; uses committed embeddings (no API key needed for querying)"
        - "ivd find-artifacts and ivd check-placement: discover existing intent artifacts in a repo; verify they follow canonical placement rules"
        - "Integration with CI/CD: running ivd validate in pre-commit hooks or CI pipelines to catch structural issues before merge"

    - id: "20.6"
      title: "Future Standards: Agent Portability and JSON Schema"
      required_content:
        - "The portability promise: intent artifacts are YAML — they work across AI assistants, IDEs, and toolchains without lock-in"
        - "JSON Schema for IVD artifacts: formalizing the intent artifact structure so any tool can validate it; schema versioning and backward compatibility"
        - "Agent portability: how intent artifacts enable switching between AI assistants (Claude, GPT, Gemini, local models) without losing project understanding"
        - "Standards evolution: where IVD fits in the broader AI tooling ecosystem; potential integration with emerging standards (MCP protocol evolution, AI IDE standards)"
        - "What stays stable vs what evolves: the 8 principles are stable; recipes, tools, and integrations evolve; the intent artifact structure evolves carefully with backward compatibility"

    - id: "20.7"
      title: "Join the IVD Community"
      required_content:
        - "Community channels: where to find IVD practitioners, ask questions, share experiences"
        - "Getting started checklist: (1) install IVD CLI or MCP server, (2) run ivd init on a project, (3) write your first intent artifact, (4) validate it, (5) join the community, (6) share your first recipe"
        - "The community ethos: IVD is built by practitioners for practitioners; every recipe started as someone's real project; contributio

tools, and integrations evolve; the intent artifact structure evolves carefully with backward compatibility"

    - id: "20.7"
      title: "Join the IVD Community"
      required_content:
        - "Community channels: where to find IVD practitioners, ask questions, share experiences"
        - "Getting started checklist: (1) install IVD CLI or MCP server, (2) run ivd init on a project, (3) write your first intent artifact, (4) validate it, (5) join the community, (6) share your first recipe"
        - "The community ethos: IVD is built by practitioners for practitioners; every recipe started as someone's real project; contribution is how the ecosystem grows"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 21: 'Now that you're part of the ecosystem, let's look at where IVD and human-AI collaboration are heading'"

  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch20 key_points: community, IDE integration, MCP server case study, CLI tools, future standards"
      - "Getting-started checklist for community participation in section 20.7 (constraint: checklist_included)"
      - "The IVD MCP server interface section shown inline or referenced (section 20.4) — all 15 tools with parameters and test paths"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 21 (future of IVD)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch20; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Community recipes and contributions — the IVD recipe repository as a living, curated collection of validated patterns"
  - "Integration with Cursor, Cline, Aider, GitHub Copilot — IVD works in any AI-assisted development environment through consistent configuration patterns"
  - "IVD tools and automation (CLI, MCP server) — validate, scaffold, search, and assess coverage from terminal or AI agent"
  - "The IVD MCP server as a real-world case study: 15 tools documented via the interface section (dogfooding IVD) — the strongest form of framework validation is using it on your own tools"
  - "Future roadmap and standards — agent portability, JSON Schema, standards evolution; what stays stable (principles) vs what evolves (tools, recipes)"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and a pre-move-on checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep 'checklist\\|Before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"

  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable,

INTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and a pre-move-on checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep 'checklist\\|Before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"

  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"

  - name: "writing_style_part_4"
    requirement: "Prose leads; examples illustrate. Part IV is about adoption — the reader already knows IVD; now they learn to integrate, contribute, and scale."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_4"
    validation: "Manual: prose leads each section; code/YAML examples support the narrative rather than drive it"
    test: "Each section in chapter.md opens with prose (not a code block); code/YAML blocks appear after explanatory context, not before"

  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths, commands, and tool names; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"

  - name: "checklist_included"
    requirement: "Section 20.7 includes a getting-started checklist for community participation (install, init, write, validate, join, share)"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways (extended for ecosystem chapter)"
    validation: "chapter.md section 20.7 contains a numbered or bulleted checklist"
    test: "grep -A 20 '20.7' chapter.md contains checklist items (numbered or bulleted list with action verbs)"

  - name: "tool_accuracy"
    requirement: "IVD MCP server tools described in section 20.4 must match the actual mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml interface section — all 15 tool names, parameter types, and descriptions must be accurate"
    source: "mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (interface section)"
    validation: "Cross-reference: every tool mentioned in 20.4 exists in mcp_server_intent.yaml interface.tools; no tool is missing or misnamed"
    test: "Diff tool names in chapter.md section 20.4 against mcp_server_intent.yaml interface.tools — zero mismatches"

  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch20 aligns with

meter types, and descriptions must be accurate"
    source: "mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (interface section)"
    validation: "Cross-reference: every tool mentioned in 20.4 exists in mcp_server_intent.yaml interface.tools; no tool is missing or misnamed"
    test: "Diff tool names in chapter.md section 20.4 against mcp_server_intent.yaml interface.tools — zero mismatches"

  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch20 aligns with framework principles and book structure; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, optional section, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"

  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks returns 0 matches"

  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "No statistics or quantitative claims without explicit source or 'illustrative' caveat"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Any quantitative claim in chapter.md is attributed or marked as illustrative"
    test: "All numeric claims (percentages, counts, metrics) in chapter.md either cite a source or include 'illustrative'/'author experience' caveat"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 20 covers the IVD ecosystem — community, IDE integration, MCP server case study, CLI tools, and future standards. Section 20.4 is the centerpiece: the IVD MCP server as a dogfooding case study."

  reasoning: |
    - Part IV purpose: adoption and scaling. Ch20 is the ecosystem chapter — after learning IVD (Parts I–III), the reader needs to know what tools exist, how to integrate them, and how to participate.
    - The IVD MCP server is the strongest possible case study because it dogfoods IVD: the server's own intent artifact uses the interface section to document its 15-tool surface. This demonstrates that IVD isn't just theory — it's how the IVD project itself operates.
    - IDE integration matters because IVD works best as ambient knowledge, not a separate step. Covering Cursor, Cline, Aider, and Copilot ensures broad applicability.
    - The CLI and MCP server provide two complementary access paths: terminal for developers who prefer command-line workflows, MCP for AI agents that need programmatic access.
    - Future standards section positions IVD in the evolving AI tooling landscape without making promises — what stays stable (principles) vs what evolves (tools, integrations).
    - The community section (20.1, 20.2, 20.7) frames I

isn't just theory — it's how the IVD project itself operates.
    - IDE integration matters because IVD works best as ambient knowledge, not a separate step. Covering Cursor, Cline, Aider, and Copilot ensures broad applicability.
    - The CLI and MCP server provide two complementary access paths: terminal for developers who prefer command-line workflows, MCP for AI agents that need programmatic access.
    - Future standards section positions IVD in the evolving AI tooling landscape without making promises — what stays stable (principles) vs what evolves (tools, integrations).
    - The community section (20.1, 20.2, 20.7) frames IVD as a living ecosystem, not a finished product. Every reader is a potential contributor.

  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_4, Ch20 key_points, placeholder); mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (15 tools, interface section); framework.md (IVD principles)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Split MCP server case study into its own chapter"
    rejected_because: "The MCP server is part of the ecosystem, not a standalone topic. Extracting it would leave the ecosystem chapter thin and create a chapter that's too focused on one tool."
    when_it_works: "If the MCP server case study grows beyond 15 pages and needs its own space to cover architecture, deployment, and extension in depth."

  - name: "Cover only Cursor integration, skip other IDEs"
    rejected_because: "IVD's value proposition is IDE-agnostic. Covering only Cursor would make IVD look tool-specific. Readers using Cline, Aider, or Copilot would feel excluded."
    when_it_works: "If the book is explicitly branded as a Cursor-specific guide, which it is not."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "MCP server tools change between writing and publication — section 20.4 becomes stale"
    action: "Reference mcp_server_intent.yaml as source of truth; keep tool list in chapter as a snapshot with version reference; note that the canonical list lives in the intent artifact"
    monitor: "mcp_server_intent.yaml changelog — any tool additions, removals, or renames require chapter update"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "IDE integration instructions become outdated as Cursor, Cline, Aider, and Copilot evolve"
    action: "Keep integration instructions at pattern level (what to configure, why) rather than step-by-step screenshots; reference official documentation for current setup details"
    monitor: "IDE release notes and configuration format changes"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Community infrastructure (recipe repository, contribution workflow) doesn't exist at publication time"
    action: "Frame community sections as the intended model with clear structure; acknowledge early-stage status if needed; provide the checklist so readers can start contributing as soon

become outdated as Cursor, Cline, Aider, and Copilot evolve"
    action: "Keep integration instructions at pattern level (what to configure, why) rather than step-by-step screenshots; reference official documentation for current setup details"
    monitor: "IDE release notes and configuration format changes"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Community infrastructure (recipe repository, contribution workflow) doesn't exist at publication time"
    action: "Frame community sections as the intended model with clear structure; acknowledge early-stage status if needed; provide the checklist so readers can start contributing as soon as infrastructure is available"
    monitor: "Community adoption intent (_private/community/community_adoption_intent.yaml) progress"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Chapter becomes a feature catalog instead of a coherent narrative — reader loses the 'why' behind each tool"
    action: "Part IV writing style: prose leads, examples illustrate. Each section must open with WHY before showing WHAT. The MCP server case study (20.4) provides narrative cohesion — dogfooding is the thread that ties the ecosystem together"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'this felt like a product page' or 'I lost track of why this matters'"
    severity: "medium"

# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:

  output: "manuscript/part-4-adoption/chapter-20-ivd-ecosystem/chapter.md"
  status: "pending"
  version: 1

  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    pending_steps:
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent, MCP server intent, IVD framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:

    - check: "Section 20.4 presents the IVD MCP server as a dogfooding case study with all 15 tools documented"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[20.4], constraints.tool_accuracy"
    - check: "All 15 tool names in section 20.4 match mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml interface.tools exactly"
      validates: "constraints.tool_accuracy"
    - check: "IDE integration

eliverables.sections (20.1–20.7) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary block and getting-started checklist"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways, constraints.checklist_included"
    - check: "Section 20.4 presents the IVD MCP server as a dogfooding case study with all 15 tools documented"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[20.4], constraints.tool_accuracy"
    - check: "All 15 tool names in section 20.4 match mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml interface.tools exactly"

    - check: "Getting-started checklist in 20.7 has actionable steps (install, init, write, validate, join, share)"
      validates: "constraints.checklist_included"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Initial chapter intent for Chapter 20 'IVD Ecosystem and Community'.
      Derived from book_system_intent.yaml Ch20 (key_points, placeholder),
      mcp_server/mcp_server_intent.yaml (15 tools, interface section), framework.md (IVD principles),
      and templates/intent.yaml (canonical structure).
      Sections 20.1–20.7 with required_content, constraints, rationale, alternatives, risks, verification.
      Part IV writing style applied (prose leads, examples illustrate).
      Key case study: IVD MCP server dogfooding — section 20.4 documents all 15 tools from the
      mcp_server_intent.yaml interface section as a real-world example of IVD applied to IVD's own tooling.
      Chapter-specific constraints: checklist_included (20.7), tool_accuracy (20.4 vs mcp_server_intent.yaml).
      Risks identified: tool staleness, IDE instruction decay, missing community infrastructure, catalog-feel.
    reason: "IVD: one intent per chapter; Ch20 is the ecosystem chapter. Intent-only; manuscript pending."
